final_answer:
Plain English: I cleaned the **active bridge/deploy/test flow** so it behaves like Gold/Gilt Chain and does not keep using old Polygon/Matic assumptions in day-to-day operations. I did **not** fully scrub every old inherited reference yet.

What I changed:
- The bridge scripts now use your **actual running chain identity** instead of a hardcoded old one.
- The scripts now prefer your new testnet/live folders first, with old folder names only as fallback so nothing breaks suddenly.
- User-facing wording in the bridge run/test/status scripts was changed from old network naming to neutral “child chain/testnet” wording.
- Main-chain bridge help text/comments were cleaned to remove Polygon-style wording where it affects operator understanding.

What is still pending (for your “full rename except Bor/Heimdall” goal):
- Old branding still exists inside inherited upstream contract stack/content (especially POS-contract package and some legacy recovery contract text).
- Some dependency links/import names still point to old upstream org names.
- So this is **not yet a full repo-wide brand scrub**.

Security-wise:
- What I did reduces “wrong-chain/wrong-config” operational mistakes.
- It does **not** equal a full anti-hack hardening pass yet.
- Next required step is full cleanup + then full test/audit pass (permissions, mint/burn/bridge routes, replay/finality checks, and end-to-end bridge drills) before calling it secure.
prompt: Wait, wait, wait, hold on. Explain non-technically what you did because I wanted to move all the references to Polygon or Matic from this and put references to Guild Chain. The bore and heimdall could stay the same in the naming but the rest, like in the code or the naming of the files, etc., everything must change. Explain to me what all you changed. I don't want it to be susceptible to vulnerabilities and attacks and hacks so explain to me what you did and what needs to be done.
